feat: implement projab.el - project-aware tab-bar with session management

desktop.el with frame restore disabled.

commit.message:
fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

Previously, only the first synced candidate was tried. If its LRC file
was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the
entire repo was skipped.

Now iterates all candidates (synced first, preserving order) until one
succeeds. Skips paths containing null bytes. Only gives up when all
candidates fail.

Recovers ~24% of repos that were previously lost.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(security): eliminate script injection vulnerabilities in action.yml (#85)

* Initial plan

* fix(security): prevent script injection in action.yml

- Pass `inputs.atmos-config-path` through env var and quote it in
  `realpath` call to prevent command injection
- Pass `inputs.base-ref` and `inputs.skip-checkout` through env vars
  and use a bash array in the `git checkout` call to prevent injection
- Remove the `affected-args` intermediate step that built a raw shell
  string from unquoted `${{ inputs.* }}` expressions
- Replace `eval` in both `atmos describe affected` invocations with
  bash arrays built from env vars, eliminating arbitrary code execution
  via user-controlled inputs (`atmos-stack`, `identity`, etc.)

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(security): DRY up repeated args-building logic between affected steps

Extract shared argument construction into a dedicated 'Build atmos
affected command args' step that writes args (one per line) to
${RUNNER_TEMP}/atmos-affected-args. Both 'affected-pro' and 'affected'
steps then read them back with mapfile, passing only the step-specific
flag (--upload or --file=) inline. No eval, no injection surface.

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

point to the Tailscale IP rather than the public VPS IP.

commit.message:
feat: scan process resilience — phase-independent recovery with retry

Critical fix: Phase 1 timeout/failure no longer terminates all phases.

Architecture changes:
- Phase 1 (scan) wrapped in independent try-catch — failure shows
  warning and continues to Phase 2/3 with existing pending tasks
- Phase 2/3: per-task retry loop (2 attempts, 3s delay between)
- Server-unreachable detection with auto-reconnection wait (30s)
- scanInProgress lock prevents duplicate concurrent scans
- Each phase queries server-side task status independently:
  Phase 2 works on enrichmentStatus=pending regardless of Phase 1
  Phase 3 works on enrichmentStatus=enriched regardless of Phase 2
- Live frozen status shows retry progress on task cards

All 7 verification checks passed. CHANGELOG updated with v2.9.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
